channel.title: LIBRCHAIN 区块链新闻雷达
channel.description: 每小时自动更新的区块链新闻、博客与 X 热点聚合

item.title: The $292 million Kelp exploit: how it happened, and what it means for DeFi
item.description: 2026 is shaping up to be DeFi's "worst year in terms of hacks," Ledger's CTO said, as the Kelp exploit shows how a single point of failure can cascade across systems.

item.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
item.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

item.title: Stablecoins not a threat to banks in the near-term: Moody's analyst
item.description: A prohibition on yield-bearing stablecoins and robust payments infrastructure in the US means stablecoins will not eat into banks' market share.

item.title: Kelp DAO Exploit Sparks Aave Liquidity Crunch, $6.2 Billion Withdrawal Panic
item.description: After attackers drained $291 million in crypto from Kelp DAO-linked infrastructure, DeFi users struggled on Aave to withdraw funds.

item.title: 2024 BTC cycle 'dramatically' underperforming previous halvings: Analyst
item.description: Volatility and upside have been declining with each Bitcoin halving cycle, but the new dynamics may not be permanent, according to Galaxy's Alex Thorn.

item.title: Previewing Consensus' Policy Summit: State of Crypto
item.description: Every year policy discussions get more important, and this year's even more so.

item.title: Web3 VCs have a differentiation problem
item.description: When every fund claims the same great networks and strong relationships, nobody wins. Bauer, Co-Founder of TBV, offers a more rigorous framework for emerging managers to build a unique value proposition.

item.title: 'DeFi is dead': crypto community scrambles after this year's biggest hack exposes contagion risks
item.description: Developers and traders warn of structural risks as a cross-chain exploit spreads fear and prompts billions to flee DeFi platforms.

item.title: The 10 Public Companies With the Biggest Bitcoin Portfolios
item.description: Companies like Strategy, Twenty One, and Metaplanet hold billions of dollars' worth of Bitcoin. These are the biggest publicly traded whales.

item.title: Kelp exploit highlights problem with non-isolated DeFi lending: Crypto execs
item.description: The contagion from the Kelp exploit could have been contained, but at the cost of capital efficiency, according to the founder of Curve Finance.

item.title: AI Traffic to US Retailers Jumps 393% in Q1 as Agentic Shoppers Outspend Humans
item.description: AI-driven traffic to U.S. retail sites surged in early 2026, and those visitors are generating more revenue than regular shoppers.

item.title: Stablecoins can help businesses turn costs into revenue, Paxos Labs cofounder says
item.description: Firms using stablecoins can reshape margins by cutting costs, unlock credit and earn yield, but not every company needs to issue a token, Paxos Labs' Chunda McCain said.

item.title: Aave sees $6 billion deposit drop as Kelp hack exposes structural risk for DeFi lender
item.description: The AAVE token fell 16% and deposits fled the protocol after attackers used drained rsETH as collateral to borrow wrapped ether, leaving Aave to quantify how much bad debt it is now carrying.

item.title: RaveDAO's RAVE token collapses 90% in a day as exchange probes widen
item.description: The token traded at $1.15 on Sunday, down from a $27.33 peak on Friday, wiping out more than $5 billion in market cap as investigations into Binance and Bitget advance.

item.title: Inside the rise of wrench attacks against crypto holders and how France has become the focus
item.description: France has seen 41 crypto-related kidnappings this year, roughly one every 2.5 days, prompting authorities to step up security.

item.title: Nomura study says 65% of institutional investors see crypto as a vital portfolio diversifier
item.description: A new survey from Nomura and Laser Digital shows improving sentiment among institutional investors, as regulatory clarity and new products drive deeper engagement with digital assets.

item.title: Bitcoin price drops to $75K as new Hormuz closure puts focus on oil
item.description: Bitcoin foreshadows fresh market mayhem as it appears that the US-Iran war has returned, including the closure of the Strait of Hormuz oil route.

item.title: One person holds the keys to $200 million of a project’s crypto. His co-founder says that has to end
item.description: Co-founders Da Hongfei and Erik Zhang have conflicting restructuring plans, Da wants independent governance and multi-sig protection; Zhang wants accountability investigations and to remain on the board.

item.title: Aluminum giant Alcoa to sell dormant smelter to Bitcoin miner NYDIG: Report
item.description: Alcoa is nearing a deal to sell its idle upstate New York Massena East smelter to NYDIG as US industrial sites increasingly shift toward Bitcoin mining and AI data centers.

item.title: RaveDAO denies manipulation as Binance, Bitget probe RAVE trading activity
item.description: RaveDAO denies involvement in RAVE token price surge and crash as Binance and Bitget launch investigations into suspected market manipulation.

item.title: Charles Schwab, Citadel Securities weigh entering prediction markets
item.description: Charles Schwab and Citadel Securities executives have separately aired interest in prediction markets, but each is looking to steer clear of sports offerings.

item.title: Caitlyn Jenner escapes memecoin lawsuit as judge says token not a security
item.description: A federal judge has tossed a class lawsuit over Caitlyn Jenner’s memecoin, saying it did not plausibly allege that the token was an unregistered security.

item.title: Warren claims SEC’s Atkins likely misled Congress over enforcement data
item.description: SEC chair Paul Atkins is under fire from Senator Elizabeth Warren, who says he “may have been deliberately trying” to mislead Congress about the agency’s enforcement activity.

item.title: Kelp restaking platform exploited, $293M drained in attack
item.description: The attack triggered a "cross-protocol contagion" that has hit at least nine crypto protocols, blockchain security firm Cyvers said.

item.title: 2026's biggest crypto exploit: $292 million gets drained from Kelp DAO with wrapped ether stranded across 20 chains
item.description: An attacker drained 116,500 rsETH, roughly 18% of circulating supply, from Kelp's LayerZero-powered bridge on Saturday, triggering emergency freezes across Aave, SparkLend, Fluid and Upshift.

item.title: Solana futures open interest rose by 20% this week: Is $100 SOL next?
item.description: SOL’s steady recovery alongside the wider crypto market has traders debating whether $100 could be the next stop for the altcoin.

item.title: Why Michael Saylor's Strategy decided to make STRC's dividend bi-monthly
item.description: Strategy aim to reduce volatility, enable consistent bitcoin buying, and create the only bi-monthly paying preferred shares in the market.

item.title: From smelters to servers: Alcoa to cash in on crypto’s thirst for energy
item.description: Alcoa is in advanced negotiations to sell its dormant Massena East smelter in upstate New York to Bitcoin mining firm NYDIG.

item.title: Bitcoin mining difficulty falls, but projected to rise in next adjustment
item.description: The average block time at publication is about 9.8 minutes, falling slightly short of the 10-minute block target, according to CoinWarz.

item.title: GalaxyOne Head Wants Retail Investors to Stake More, Predict Less
item.description: Zac Prince, head of Galaxy’s retail platform, said he struggles to see prediction markets in diversified portfolios for long-term investors.

item.title: AI is increasingly eating into VC fundings and here is how crypto firms are adapting
item.description: AI companies raised $242 billion (80% of global venture funding) in early 2026, with Gartner projecting total AI spending will reach $2.52 trillion this year.

item.title: Iran views BTC as a strategic asset, but USDt still dominates oil tolls: BPI
item.description: The Iranian government chose Bitcoin as a payment method for oil tolls due to its confiscation-resistant properties, but only dollar stablecoins have been used so far.

item.title: Binance and Bitget to probe RAVE’s 4,500% token surge as claims of insider-orchestrated rally grow
item.description: Nearly 90% of RAVE's supply was concentrated in just three wallets, and millions of tokens were transferred to exchanges before the price surge.

item.title: The Lightning Network isn’t ‘helplessly broken’
item.description: Shell argues the network is fixable and proposes a different framing to the recent quantum debate.

item.title: What Is Q-Day? The Quantum Threat to Bitcoin Explained
item.description: Experts warn quantum computers could someday forge Bitcoin’s digital signatures, allowing unauthorized transactions.

item.title: Zondacrypto under fire as Poland's prime minister links exchange to legislative interference
item.description: The company also disclosed a 4,500 BTC wallet that it can't access due to missing private keys linked to a former CEO, who's now missing.

item.title: Bitcoin 'plebs eat first' mining pool Parasite finds its second BTC block
item.description: Parasite Pool, which pays 1 BTC to the block finder and splits the rest among all participants, mined block 945,601 on Friday, about 48 days after its first.

item.title: OpenAI's New AI Model Rosalind Could Shave Years Off Drug Discovery. You Probably Can't Use It
item.description: GPT-Rosalind is OpenAI's first domain-specific model, built for drug discovery and life sciences—and it's not for everyone.

item.title: Wrapped XRP goes live on Solana, broadening DeFi access for Ripple-linked token
item.description: Wrapped XRP on Solana lets XRP holders access Jupiter, Phantom, and Meteora without selling the asset, the latest step in Hex Trust's multi-chain wXRP rollout first announced in December.

item.title: Ethereum co-founder Joseph Lubin warns of the dangers of AI being controlled by a few big tech firms
item.description: In an interview with CoinDesk, the Ethereum co-founder spoke also about Ethereum’s evolution through MetaMask, stablecoins and tokenization, while downplaying quantum computing as a long-term, manageable issue.

item.title: Poland parliament fails again to override presidential veto on crypto bill
item.description: Poland’s parliament has once again failed to overturn President Karol Nawrocki’s veto of the crypto regulation bill.

item.title: Stablecoins behave like FX markets as liquidity splits: Eco CEO
item.description: Stablecoins promise seamless dollar movement, but fragmented liquidity is turning large transfers into complex execution problems, says Eco CEO Ryne Saxe.

item.title: Spot Bitcoin ETFs attract nearly $1B in weekly inflows as risk sentiment improves
item.description: Spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded nearly $1 billion in weekly inflows, their strongest performance in over three months amid improving risk sentiment.

item.title: SEC charges Donald Basile in $16M crypto fraud tied to ‘insured’ token
item.description: The SEC has sued crypto executive Donald Basile over an alleged $16 million scheme involving false claims about an “insured” Bitcoin Latinum token.

item.title: Circle unveils USDC Bridge for native cross-chain stablecoin transfers
item.description: The USDC Bridge adds to Circle's Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol, which often sees over $500 million worth of USDC transfers each day.

item.title: X's new Cashtags feature drives $1B trading volume in first two days
item.description: X’s Cashtags feature has been integrated into Canadian-based online brokerage Wealthsimple, but has not yet been adopted by a US trading platform.

item.title: Sanctioned Russia-Linked Exchange Grinex Suspends Operations Following Alleged Cyberattack
item.description: TL;DR Grinex, the sanctioned successor to the Russian exchange Garantex, suspended operations yesterday following a claimed 1 billion ruble ($13.7… The post Sanctioned Russia-Linked Exchange Grinex Suspends Operations Following Alleged Cyb…

item.title: Bitcoin Cracks 7-Month Ceiling. Can Bulls Push It Higher?
item.description: The price of Bitcoin breaks a seven-month downtrend as geopolitical shifts and prediction markets point to $84K next.

item.title: You Can Now Use XRP on Solana—Here's How
item.description: More than $1.2 million worth of wrapped XRP tokens (wXRP) have been minted on Solana as the Ripple-linked asset gains greater DeFi utility.

item.title: Rep. Sheri Biggs Doubles Down on Bitcoin, Buys Up to $250K of BlackRock's ETF
item.description: Rep. Sheri Biggs purchased up to $250,000 worth of BlackRock's spot Bitcoin ETF last month, padding a position she entered into last July.

item.title: Sam Altman's World Teams With Zoom, Tinder to Better Verify Humans in the AI Age
item.description: Iris-scanning crypto project World expands with Tinder's U.S. human verification rollout and Zoom's Deep Face feature.

item.title: Elizabeth Warren Accuses SEC Chair Paul Atkins of Potentially Lying to Congress
item.description: Senator Elizabeth Warren said Paul Atkins may have intentionally misled Congress by pushing back over the SEC's dwindling enforcement actions.

item.title: Crypto Trader Turns $2,500 Into $500K on Skyrocketing Ethereum Meme Coin
item.description: A meme coin trader turned $2,500 into nearly $500,000 in a matter of hours via the Elon Musk-linked ASTEROID token on Ethereum.

item.title: Anthropic’s Alarming Mythos Findings Replicated With Off-the-Shelf AI, Researchers Say
item.description: Security researchers used GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6 in an open-source harness to reproduce Anthropic's Mythos vulnerability findings for under $30 per scan.

item.title: Strategy Shares Pop as Bitcoin Holdings Flip Green, Near $61 Billion
item.description: The Bitcoin-buying firm’s stockpile had shown losses on paper since early February.

item.title: US Government Moves Bitcoin Tied to $9 Billion Bitfinex Hack
item.description: The U.S. government moved 8.2 Bitcoin, or greater than $600,000, that is linked to the $9 billion Bitfinex hack from 2016.

item.title: Dogecoin Gold? A DOGE-Themed Company Is Joining the Tokenized Gold Rush
item.description: The company Dogecoin Cash plans to develop tokens representing physical gold, following the lead of firms like Tether and Paxos.

item.title: AI Agents Already Run a Fifth of DeFi, But Still Lose to Humans at Trading
item.description: A new report finds that agents have taken over the predictable corners of DeFi, but humans still clear the table when trades get complex.

item.title: Bitcoin, Stocks Surge as Iran Says Strait of Hormuz Is 'Completely Open'
item.description: Bitcoin spiked above $77,000 as stock indices set records, following word that Iran's Strait of Hormuz is open during the ceasefire.

item.title: Kraken Parent Payward Agrees to Acquire Derivatives Exchange Bitnomial for $550 Million
item.description: The acquisition will give Kraken access to complete CFTC-licensed crypto derivatives infrastructure in the U.S.
